principle.name: Data-Driven Decision Making
principle.description: Always base visualization choices on the actual data characteristics and user needs
principle.rationale: Different data types and distributions require different visualization approaches for clarity

guidelines.guideline: Perform thorough EDA before choosing chart types
Consider the story you want the data to tell
Match visualization complexity to audience expertise

principle.name: Responsive Design
principle.description: Build interfaces that work seamlessly across all device sizes
principle.rationale: Users access analytics dashboards from various devices

guidelines.guideline: Use Tailwind responsive classes (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:)
Test on mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports
Make charts responsive with maintainAspectRatio: false
Consider touch targets for mobile users (minimum 44x44px)

principle.name: Performance Optimization
principle.description: Ensure fast load times and smooth interactions
principle.rationale: Slow dashboards frustrate users and reduce engagement

guidelines.guideline: Limit initial data loads to essential information
Lazy load charts that aren't immediately visible

convention.rule: Use Carbon Design System colors for consistency

convention.example: Title

Overview

Data Visualizations

guideline.rule: Choose the right chart type for your data

type.name: line
type.example: Sales over months, temperature changes, stock prices

type.name: bar
type.example: Sales by region, product comparisons, survey results

type.name: pie
type.use_when: Showing parts of a whole (use sparingly)

type.name: scatter
type.example: Price vs. sales, age vs. income

best_practices.practice: Limit color palette to 5-7 distinct colors
Use sequential colors for ordered data
Use diverging colors for data with a meaningful midpoint
Use descriptive axis labels with units
Position legends where they don't obscure data

pitfall.why_problematic: Complex charts confuse users and obscure insights
pitfall.correct_approach: Start simple, add complexity only when necessary

pitfall.description: Not handling edge cases in data
pitfall.why_problematic: Missing values, outliers, or empty datasets cause errors
pitfall.correct_approach: Always validate data and handle edge cases gracefully

pitfall.description: Hardcoding values instead of using configuration
pitfall.why_problematic: Makes code inflexible and hard to maintain
pitfall.correct_approach: Use configuration files or environment variables

pitfall.why_problematic: Charts may be unreadable or interactions may not work on mobile
pitfall.correct_approach: Test on mobile devices and use responsive design patterns

pitfall.why_problematic: Users can't interpret visualizations without context
pitfall.correct_approach: Include titles, descriptions, and data sources

category.name: before_starting
category.item: Understand all page requirements from the user
Have access to all necessary datasets
Know the target audience and their needs
Understand any branding or styling requirements

category.name: during_development
category.item: Test API endpoints as you build them
Verify charts render correctly with real data
Check responsive design at each breakpoint

category.name: before_completion
category.item: All pages from template are implemented
Responsive design works on mobile, tablet, desktop
Color contrast meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards
All interactive elements are keyboard accessible
Error handling works for edge cases
README includes setup and run instructions
